subject: ela
title: Essay Structure: Introduction, Body, Conclusion
content:
Essay Structure

Every well-written essay has three main parts: an Introduction, Body Paragraphs, and a Conclusion. Think of it like a sandwich — intro and conclusion are the bread, and the body is the filling!

The Introduction

The introduction catches your reader's attention and tells them what your essay will be about. It should include:

1. A hook — something interesting to start with (a question, surprising fact, or quote)
2. Background information — what readers need to know
3. Thesis statement — your main argument or point in one sentence

Example:
"Can you imagine learning about the world from a tiny seed? Gardening teaches patience (hook), demonstrates responsibility (background), and connects us to nature (thesis)."

The Body Paragraphs

Body paragraphs develop your main idea. Each paragraph should:

1. Have a topic sentence — the main idea of that paragraph
2. Include evidence — facts, examples, or quotes that support it
3. Have explanation — tell why this evidence matters

Structure: "TEEC"
• Topic sentence
• Evidence
• Explanation
• Conclusion (of the paragraph)

The Conclusion

The conclusion wraps up your essay. It should:

1. Restate your thesis in new words
2. Summarize your main points
3. End with a thought or call to action

Example:
"In conclusion, gardening teaches valuable life skills. As we've seen, it builds patience, responsibility, and connection to nature. These skills will help students throughout their lives."

Essay Writing Tips

• Start with an outline to organize your ideas
• Each paragraph should focus on ONE main idea
• Use transitions between paragraphs
• Keep your thesis in mind while writing
• Proofread for grammar and spelling

questions.prompt: What are the three main parts of an essay?
questions.hint: Think of it like a sandwich

choices: The main argument of the essay

questions.prompt: What should each body paragraph include?
What is the purpose of the introduction?
choices: To catch attention and introduce the topic
questions.hint: It should grab the reader's interest

questions.prompt: In the conclusion, you should NOT...
questions.hint: Don't bring up new ideas in the conclusion
